name: São Tomé and Príncipe Portuguese

ancestors.notes: European Portuguese colonial base. São Tomé and Príncipe was uninhabited before Portuguese settlement from 1486; among the oldest Portuguese colonial societies.

Angolan Portuguese adstrate: post-independence immigration from Angola; also, São Tomense (Forro), Angolar, and Principense are Portuguese-based creoles native to the islands — modelled here via Angolan Portuguese as the nearest registry code.
Angolan Portuguese adstrate: post-independence immigration from Angola brought Angolan Portuguese patterns to São Tomé.

notes: São Tomé and Príncipe Portuguese (Português de São Tomé e Príncipe). Official language; increasingly replacing the traditional creoles (Forro/São Tomense, Angolar, Principense) as L1 in younger generations. Unique as one of the few places where Portuguese is genuinely replacing endemic creoles. KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: no /ɨ/; full unstressed vowels as in other African varieties. (2) SIBILANTS: coda /s/ → [s], not palatalised. (3) RHOTIC: alveolar [r]. (4) OPEN VOWELS: /e/, /o/ tend to open realisations. (5) HISTORICAL DEPTH: São Tomé received enslaved Africans from across the Gulf of Guinea from the 15th century, making its creoles among the oldest Atlantic creoles; this unique history shapes the Portuguese spoken there. Sources: Ferraz (1979), Günther (1973), Hagemeijer (2009).

sources.author: Ferraz, L.I.
sources.title: The Creole of São Tomé
sources.notes: Key reference for São Tomense creole and Portuguese relationship
